   712	  def sql_for_parent_id_field(field, operator, value)
   713	    case operator
   714	    when "="
   715	      # accepts a comma separated list of ids
   716	      ids = value.first.to_s.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i).uniq
   717	      if ids.present?
   718	        "#{Issue.table_name}.parent_id IN (#{ids.join(",")})"
   719	      else
   720	        "1=0"
   721	      end
   722	    when "~"
   723	      ids = value.first.to_s.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i).uniq
   724	      conditions = ids.filter_map do |id|
   725	        root_id, lft, rgt = Issue.where(id: id).pick(:root_id, :lft, :rgt)
   726	        if root_id && lft && rgt
   727	          "(#{Issue.table_name}.root_id = #{root_id} AND #{Issue.table_name}.lft > #{lft} AND #{Issue.table_name}.rgt < #{rgt})"
   728	        else
   729	          nil
   730	        end
   731	      end
   732	
   733	      if conditions.any?
   734	        "(#{conditions.join(' OR ')})"
   735	      else
   736	        "1=0"
   737	      end
   738	    when "!*"
   739	      "#{Issue.table_name}.parent_id IS NULL"
   740	    when "*"
   741	      "#{Issue.table_name}.parent_id IS NOT NULL"
   742	    end
   743	  end
   744	
   745	  def sql_for_child_id_field(field, operator, value)
   746	    case operator
   747	    when "="
   748	      # accepts a comma separated list of child ids
   749	      child_ids = value.first.to_s.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i).uniq
   750	      ids = Issue.where(:id => child_ids).pluck(:parent_id).compact.uniq
   751	      if ids.present?
   752	        "#{Issue.table_name}.id IN (#{ids.join(",")})"
   753	      else
   754	        "1=0"
   755	      end
   756	    when "~"
   757	      root_id, lft, rgt = Issue.where(:id => value.first.to_i).pick(:root_id, :lft, :rgt)
   758	      if root_id && lft && rgt
   759	        "#{Issue.table_name}.root_id = #{root_id} AND #{Issue.table_name}.lft < #{lft} AND #{Issue.table_name}.rgt > #{rgt}"
   760	      else
   761	        "1=0"
   762	      end
   763	    when "!*"
   764	      "#{Issue.table_name}.rgt - #{Issue.table_name}.lft = 1"
   765	    when "*"
   766	      "#{Issue.table_name}.rgt - #{Issue.table_name}.lft > 1"
   767	    end
   768	  end
   769	
   770	  def sql_for_updated_on_field(field, operator, value)
   771	    case operator
   772	    when "!*"
   773	      "#{Issue.table_name}.updated_on = #{Issue.table_name}.created_on"
   774	    when "*"
   775	      "#{Issue.table_name}.updated_on > #{Issue.table_name}.created_on"
   776	    else
   777	      sql_for_field("updated_on", operator, value, Issue.table_name, "updated_on")
   778	    end
   779	  end

   781	  def sql_for_issue_id_field(field, operator, value)
   782	    if operator == "="
   783	      # accepts a comma separated list of ids
   784	      ids = value.first.to_s.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i)
   785	      if ids.present?
   786	        "#{Issue.table_name}.id IN (#{ids.join(",")})"
   787	      else
   788	        "1=0"

   795	  def sql_for_relations(field, operator, value, options={})
   796	    relation_options = IssueRelation::TYPES[field]
   797	    return relation_options unless relation_options
   798	
   799	    relation_type = field
   800	    join_column, target_join_column = "issue_from_id", "issue_to_id"
   801	    if relation_options[:reverse] || options[:reverse]
   802	      relation_type = relation_options[:reverse] || relation_type
   803	      join_column, target_join_column = target_join_column, join_column
   804	    end
   805	    sql =
   806	      case operator
   807	      when "*", "!*"
   808	        op = (operator == "*" ? 'IN' : 'NOT IN')
   809	        "#{Issue.table_name}.id #{op}" \
   810	         " (SELECT DISTINCT #{IssueRelation.table_name}.#{join_column}" \
   811	           " FROM #{IssueRelation.table_name}" \
   812	             " WHERE #{IssueRelation.table_name}.relation_type =" \
   813	                  " '#{self.class.connection.quote_string(relation_type)}')"
   814	      when "=", "!"
   815	        ids = value.first.to_s.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i).uniq
   816	        if ids.present?
   817	          op = (operator == "=" ? 'IN' : 'NOT IN')
   818	          "#{Issue.table_name}.id #{op}" \
   819	           " (SELECT DISTINCT #{IssueRelation.table_name}.#{join_column}" \
   820	             " FROM #{IssueRelation.table_name}" \
   821	               " WHERE #{IssueRelation.table_name}.relation_type =" \
   822	                    " '#{self.class.connection.quote_string(relation_type)}'" \
   823	                 " AND #{IssueRelation.table_name}.#{target_join_column} IN (#{ids.join(",")}))"
   824	        else
   825	          "1=0"
   826	        end
   827	      when "=p", "=!p", "!p"
   828	        op = (operator == "!p" ? 'NOT IN' : 'IN')
   829	        comp = (operator == "=!p" ? '<>' : '=')
   830	        "#{Issue.table_name}.id #{op}" \
   831	         " (SELECT DISTINCT #{IssueRelation.table_name}.#{join_column}" \
   832	           " FROM #{IssueRelation.table_name}, #{Issue.table_name} relissues" \
   833	             " WHERE #{IssueRelation.table_name}.relation_type =" \
   834	                  " '#{self.class.connection.quote_string(relation_type)}'" \
   835	             " AND #{IssueRelation.table_name}.#{target_join_column} = relissues.id" \
   836	             " AND relissues.project_id #{comp} #{value.first.to_i})"
   837	      when "*o", "!o"
   838	        op = (operator == "!o" ? 'NOT IN' : 'IN')
   839	        "#{Issue.table_name}.id #{op}" \
   840	          " (SELECT DISTINCT #{IssueRelation.table_name}.#{join_column}" \
   841	           " FROM #{IssueRelation.table_name}, #{Issue.table_name} relissues" \
   842	             " WHERE #{IssueRelation.table_name}.relation_type =" \
   843	                  " '#{self.class.connection.quote_string(relation_type)}'" \
   844	             " AND #{IssueRelation.table_name}.#{target_join_column} = relissues.id" \
   845	             " AND relissues.status_id IN" \
   846	               " (SELECT id FROM #{IssueStatus.table_name}" \
   847	               "  WHERE is_closed = #{self.class.connection.quoted_false}))"
   848	      end
   849	    if relation_options[:sym] == field && !options[:reverse]
   850	      sqls = [sql, sql_for_relations(field, operator, value, :reverse => true)]
   851	      sql = sqls.join(["!", "!*", "!p", '!o'].include?(operator) ? " AND " : " OR ")
   852	    end
   853	    "(#{sql})"
   854	  end

   860	  def sql_for_any_searchable_field(field, operator, value)
   861	    question = value.first
   862	
   863	    # Fetch search results only from the selected and visible (sub-)projects
   864	    project_scope = Project.allowed_to(:view_issues)
   865	    if project
   866	      projects = project_scope.where(project_statement)
   867	    elsif has_filter?('project_id')
   868	      case values_for('project_id').first
   869	      when 'mine'
   870	        project_ids = User.current.projects.ids
   871	      when 'bookmarks'
   872	        project_ids = User.current.bookmarked_project_ids
   873	      else
   874	        project_ids = values_for('project_id')
   875	      end
   876	      projects = project_scope.where(
   877	        sql_for_field('project_id', operator_for('project_id'), project_ids, Project.table_name, 'id')
   878	      )
   879	    else
   880	      projects = nil
   881	    end
   882	
   883	    is_all_words =
   884	      case operator
   885	      when '~'        then true
   886	      when '*~', '!~' then false
   887	      end
   888	
   889	    is_open_issues = has_filter?('status_id') && operator_for('status_id') == 'o'
   890	
   891	    fetcher = Redmine::Search::Fetcher.new(
   892	      question, User.current, ['issue'], projects,
   893	      all_words: is_all_words, open_issues: is_open_issues, attachments: '0'
   894	    )
   895	    ids = fetcher.result_ids.map(&:last)
   896	    if ids.present?
   897	      sw = operator == '!~' ? 'NOT' : ''
   898	      "#{Issue.table_name}.id #{sw} IN (#{ids.join(',')})"
   899	    else
   900	      operator == '!~' ? '1=1' : '1=0'
   901	    end
   902	  end

   909	  IssueRelation::TYPES.each_key do |relation_type|

   913	  def joins_for_order_statement(order_options)
   914	    joins = [super]
   915	
   916	    if order_options
   917	      if order_options.include?('authors')
   918	        joins << "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{User.table_name} authors ON authors.id = #{queried_table_name}.author_id"
   919	      end
   920	      if order_options.include?('users')
   921	        joins << "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{User.table_name} ON #{User.table_name}.id = #{queried_table_name}.assigned_to_id"
   922	      end
   923	      if order_options.include?('last_journal_user')
   924	        joins <<
   925	           "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{Journal.table_name}" \
   926	             " ON #{Journal.table_name}.id = (SELECT MAX(#{Journal.table_name}.id)" \
   927	             " FROM #{Journal.table_name}" \
   928	             " WHERE #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_type = 'Issue'" \
   929	             " AND #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id " \
   930	             " AND #{Journal.visible_notes_condition(User.current, :skip_pre_condition => true)})" \
   931	             " LEFT OUTER JOIN #{User.table_name} last_journal_user" \
   932	             " ON last_journal_user.id = #{Journal.table_name}.user_id"
   933	      end
   934	      if order_options.include?('versions')
   935	        joins <<
   936	           "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{Version.table_name}" \
   937	             " ON #{Version.table_name}.id = #{queried_table_name}.fixed_version_id"
   938	      end
   939	      if order_options.include?('issue_categories')
   940	        joins <<
   941	           "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{IssueCategory.table_name}" \
   942	             " ON #{IssueCategory.table_name}.id = #{queried_table_name}.category_id"
   943	      end
   944	      if order_options.include?('trackers')
   945	        joins <<
   946	          "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{Tracker.table_name}" \
   947	            " ON #{Tracker.table_name}.id = #{queried_table_name}.tracker_id"
   948	      end
   949	      if order_options.include?('enumerations')
   950	        joins <<
   951	          "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{IssuePriority.table_name}" \
   952	            " ON #{IssuePriority.table_name}.id = #{queried_table_name}.priority_id"
   953	      end
   954	    end
   955	
   956	    joins.any? ? joins.join(' ') : nil
   957	  end
   958	end

  1571	  # Returns an issue scope based on project and scope
  1572	  def self.cross_project_scope(project, scope=nil)
  1573	    if project.nil?
  1574	      return Issue
  1575	    end
  1576	
  1577	    case scope
  1578	    when 'all', 'system'
  1579	      Issue
  1580	    when 'tree'
  1581	      Issue.joins(:project).where("(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt)",
  1582	                                  :lft => project.root.lft, :rgt => project.root.rgt)
  1583	    when 'hierarchy'
  1584	      Issue.joins(:project).
  1585	        where(
  1586	          "(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND " \
  1587	            "#{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt) OR " \
  1588	            "(#{Project.table_name}.lft < :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt > :rgt)",
  1589	          :lft => project.lft, :rgt => project.rgt
  1590	        )
  1591	    when 'descendants'
  1592	      Issue.joins(:project).where("(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt)",
  1593	                                  :lft => project.lft, :rgt => project.rgt)
  1594	    else
  1595	      Issue.where(:project_id => project.id)
  1596	    end
  1597	  end

  1628	  # Query generator for selecting groups of issue counts for a project
  1629	  # based on specific criteria
  1630	  #
  1631	  # Options
  1632	  # * project - Project to search in.
  1633	  # * with_subprojects - Includes subprojects issues if set to true.
  1634	  # * association - Symbol. Association for grouping.
  1635	  def self.count_and_group_by(options)
  1636	    assoc = reflect_on_association(options[:association])
  1637	    select_field = assoc.foreign_key
  1638	
  1639	    Issue.
  1640	      visible(User.current, :project => options[:project], :with_subprojects => options[:with_subprojects]).
  1641	      joins(:status).
  1642	      group(:status_id, :is_closed, select_field).
  1643	      count.
  1644	      map do |columns, total|
  1645	        status_id, is_closed, field_value = columns
  1646	        is_closed = ['t', 'true', '1'].include?(is_closed.to_s)
  1647	        {
  1648	          "status_id" => status_id.to_s,
  1649	          "closed" => is_closed,
  1650	          select_field => field_value.to_s,
  1651	          "total" => total.to_s
  1652	        }
  1653	      end
  1654	  end

  1656	  # Returns a scope of projects that user can assign the subtask
  1657	  def allowed_target_projects_for_subtask(user=User.current)
  1658	    if parent_issue_id.present?
  1659	      scope = filter_projects_scope(Setting.cross_project_subtasks)
  1660	    end
  1661	
  1662	    self.class.allowed_target_projects(user, project, scope)
  1663	  end
  1664	
  1665	  # Returns a scope of projects that user can assign the issue to
  1666	  def allowed_target_projects(user=User.current, scope=nil)
  1667	    current_project = new_record? ? nil : project
  1668	    if scope
  1669	      scope = filter_projects_scope(scope)
  1670	    end
  1671	
  1672	    self.class.allowed_target_projects(user, current_project, scope)
  1673	  end
  1674	
  1675	  # Returns a scope of projects that user can assign issues to
  1676	  # If current_project is given, it will be included in the scope
  1677	  def self.allowed_target_projects(user=User.current, current_project=nil, scope=nil)
  1678	    condition = Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :add_issues)
  1679	    if current_project
  1680	      condition = ["(#{condition}) OR #{Project.table_name}.id = ?", current_project.id]
  1681	    end
  1682	
  1683	    if scope.nil?
  1684	      scope = Project
  1685	    end
  1686	
  1687	    scope.where(condition).having_trackers
  1688	  end

The query layer is heavily coupled to Issue’s schema (`parent_id`, `root_id/lft/rgt`, journal polymorphic keys, attachments’ `container_type`, watchers, and time entry sums). I’m checking shared behavior modules and notifications/webhooks so the final map includes plugin-style contracts too.

    64	      module InstanceMethods
    65	        def self.included(base)
    66	          base.extend ClassMethods
    67	        end
    68	
    69	        module ClassMethods
    70	          # Searches the model for the given tokens and user visibility.
    71	          # The projects argument can be either nil (will search all projects), a project or an array of projects.
    72	          # Returns an array that contains the rank and id of all results.
    73	          # In current implementation, the rank is the record timestamp converted as an integer.
    74	          #
    75	          # Valid options:
    76	          # * :titles_only - searches tokens in the first searchable column only
    77	          # * :all_words - searches results that match all token
    78	          # * :
    79	          # * :limit - maximum number of results to return
    80	          #
    81	          # Example:
    82	          #   Issue.search_result_ranks_and_ids("foo")
    83	          #   # => [[1419595329, 69], [1419595622, 123]]
    84	          def search_result_ranks_and_ids(tokens, user=User.current, projects=nil, options={})
    85	            tokens = [] << tokens unless tokens.is_a?(Array)
    86	            projects = [] << projects if projects.is_a?(Project)
    87	
    88	            columns = searchable_options[:columns]
    89	            columns = columns[0..0] if options[:titles_only]
    90	
    91	            r = []
    92	            queries = 0
    93	
    94	            unless options[:attachments] == 'only'
    95	              r = fetch_ranks_and_ids(
    96	                search_scope(user, projects, options).
    97	                where(search_tokens_condition(columns, tokens, options[:all_words])),
    98	                options[:limit]
    99	              )
   100	              queries += 1
   101	              if !options[:titles_only] && searchable_options[:search_custom_fields]
   102	                searchable_custom_fields = CustomField.where(:type => "#{self.name}CustomField", :searchable => true).to_a
   103	                if searchable_custom_fields.any?
   104	                  fields_by_visibility = searchable_custom_fields.group_by {|field|
   105	                    field.visibility_by_project_condition(searchable_options[:project_key], user, "#{CustomValue.table_name}.custom_field_id")
   106	                  }
   107	                  clauses = []
   108	                  fields_by_visibility.each do |visibility, fields|
   109	                    clauses << "(#{CustomValue.table_name}.custom_field_id IN (#{fields.map(&:id).join(',')}) AND (#{visibility}))"
   110	                  end
   111	                  visibility = clauses.join(' OR ')
   112	                  scope =
   113	                    search_scope(user, projects, options)
   114	                    .joins(:custom_values)
   115	                    .where(visibility)
   116	                    .where(search_tokens_condition(["#{CustomValue.table_name}.value"], tokens, options[:all_words]))
   117	
   118	                  if self == Issue && Redmine::Database.mysql? && ActiveRecord::Base.connection.supports_optimizer_hints?
   119	                    # Force the join order to evaluate issues before custom_values.
   120	                    # This avoids inefficient query plans where custom_values are scanned
   121	                    # without an issue_id filter, which can be extremely slow on large datasets.
   122	                    scope = scope.optimizer_hints("JOIN_ORDER(#{Issue.table_name}, #{CustomValue.table_name})")
   123	                  end
   124	
   125	                  r |= fetch_ranks_and_ids(scope, options[:limit])
   126	                  queries += 1
   127	                end
   128	              end
   129	
   130	              if !options[:titles_only] && searchable_options[:search_journals]
   131	                r |= fetch_ranks_and_ids(
   132	                  search_scope(user, projects, options).
   133	                  joins(:journals).
   134	                  where("#{Journal.table_name}.private_notes = ? OR (#{Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_private_notes)})", false).
   135	                  where(search_tokens_condition(["#{Journal.table_name}.notes"], tokens, options[:all_words])),
   136	                  options[:limit]
   137	                )
   138	                queries += 1
   139	              end
   140	            end
   141	
   142	            if searchable_options[:search_attachments] && (options[:titles_only] ? options[:attachments] == 'only' : options[:attachments] != '0')
   143	              r |= fetch_ranks_and_ids(
   144	                search_scope(user, projects, options).
   145	                joins(:attachments).
   146	                where(search_tokens_condition(["#{Attachment.table_name}.filename", "#{Attachment.table_name}.description"], tokens, options[:all_words])),
   147	                options[:limit]
   148	              )
   149	              queries += 1
   150	            end
   151	
   152	            if queries > 1
   153	              r = r.sort.reverse
   154	              if options[:limit] && r.size > options[:limit]
   155	                r = r[0, options[:limit]]
   156	              end
   157	            end
   158	
   159	            r
   160	          end

    24	    h = super

    26	      journal = journals.visible(user).find_by_id(current_journal.id)
    27	      if journal.present?
    28	        h[:data][:journal] = journal_payload(journal, user)

    41	      notes: journal.notes,
    42	      user: {
    43	        id: journal.user.id,
    44	        name: journal.user.name,
    45	      },
    46	      details: journal.visible_details(user).map do |d|
    47	        {
    48	          property: d.property,
    49	          prop_key: d.prop_key,
    50	          old_value: d.old_value,
    51	          value: d.value,
    52	        }
    53	      end
    54	    }

190	  def receive_issue
   191	    project = target_project
   192	
   193	    # Never receive emails to projects where adding issues is not possible
   194	    raise NotAllowedInProject, "not possible to add issues to project [#{project.name}]" unless project.allows_to?(:add_issues)
   195	
   196	    # check permission
   197	    unless handler_options[:no_permission_check]
   198	      raise InsufficientPermissions, "not allowed to add issues to project [#{project.name}]" unless user.allowed_to?(:add_issues, project)
   199	    end
   200	
   201	    issue = Issue.new(:author => user, :project => project)
   202	    attributes = issue_attributes_from_keywords(issue)
   203	    if handler_options[:no_permission_check]
   204	      issue.tracker_id = attributes['tracker_id']
   205	      if project
   206	        issue.tracker_id ||= project.trackers.first.try(:id)
   207	      end
   208	    end
   209	    issue.safe_attributes = attributes
   210	    issue.safe_attributes = {'custom_field_values' => custom_field_values_from_keywords(issue)}
   211	    issue.subject = cleaned_up_subject
   212	    if issue.subject.blank?
   213	      issue.subject = "(#{ll(Setting.default_language, :text_no_subject)})"
   214	    end
   215	    issue.description = cleaned_up_text_body
   216	    issue.start_date ||= User.current.today if Setting.default_issue_start_date_to_creation_date?
   217	    offset = Setting.default_issue_due_date_offset_in_days
   218	    issue.due_date ||= User.current.today + offset if offset
   219	    if handler_options[:issue][:is_private] == '1'
   220	      issue.is_private = true
   221	    end
   222	
   223	    # add To and Cc as watchers before saving so the watchers can reply to Redmine
   224	    add_watchers(issue)
   225	    issue.save!
   226	    add_attachments(issue)
   227	    logger&.info "MailHandler: issue ##{issue.id} created by #{user}"
   228	    issue
   229	  end
   230	
   231	  # Adds a note to an existing issue
   232	  def receive_issue_reply(issue_id, from_journal=nil)
   233	    issue = Issue.find_by(:id => issue_id)
   234	    if issue.nil?
   235	      raise MissingContainer, "reply to nonexistant issue [##{issue_id}]"
   236	    end
   237	
   238	    # Never receive emails to projects where adding issue notes is not possible
   239	    project = issue.project
   240	    raise NotAllowedInProject, "not possible to add notes to project [#{project.name}]" unless project.allows_to?(:add_issue_notes)
   241	
   242	    # check permission
   243	    unless handler_options[:no_permission_check]
   244	      unless issue.visible?(user) && issue.notes_addable?
   245	        raise InsufficientPermissions, "not allowed to add notes on issues to project [#{issue.project.name}]"
   246	      end
   247	    end
   248	
   249	    # ignore CLI-supplied defaults for new issues
   250	    handler_options[:issue] = {}
   251	
   252	    journal = issue.init_journal(user)
   253	    if from_journal && from_journal.private_notes?
   254	      # If the received email was a reply to a private note, make the added note private
   255	      issue.private_notes = true
   256	    end
   257	    issue.safe_attributes = issue_attributes_from_keywords(issue)

   603	  def self.reminders(options={})
   604	    days = options[:days] || 7
   605	    project = options[:project] ? Project.find(options[:project]) : nil
   606	    tracker = options[:tracker] ? Tracker.find(options[:tracker]) : nil
   607	    target_version_id = options[:version] ? Version.named(options[:version]).pluck(:id) : nil
   608	    if options[:version] && target_version_id.blank?
   609	      raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound.new("Couldn't find Version named #{options[:version]}")
   610	    end
   611	
   612	    user_ids = options[:users]
   613	
   614	    scope =
   615	      Issue.open.where(
   616	        "#{Issue.table_name}.assigned_to_id IS NOT NULL" \
   617	          " AND #{Project.table_name}.status = #{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}" \
   618	          " AND #{Issue.table_name}.due_date <= ?", days.day.from_now.to_date
   619	      )
   620	    scope = scope.where(:assigned_to_id => user_ids) if user_ids.present?
   621	    scope = scope.where(:project_id => project.id) if project
   622	    scope = scope.where(:fixed_version_id => target_version_id) if target_version_id.present?
   623	    scope = scope.where(:tracker_id => tracker.id) if tracker
   624	    issues_by_assignee = scope.includes(:status, :assigned_to, :project, :tracker).
   625	                              group_by(&:assigned_to)
   626	    issues_by_assignee.keys.each do |assignee|  # rubocop:disable Style/HashEachMethods
   627	      if assignee.is_a?(Group)
   628	        assignee.users.each do |user|
   629	          issues_by_assignee[user] ||= []
   630	          issues_by_assignee[user] += issues_by_assignee[assignee]
   631	        end
   632	      end
   633	    end
   634	
   635	    issues_by_assignee.each do |assignee, issues|
   636	      if assignee.is_a?(User) && assignee.active? && issues.present?
   637	        visible_issues = issues.select {|i| i.visible?(assignee)}
   638	        visible_issues.sort!{|a, b| (a.due_date <=> b.due_date).nonzero? || (a.id <=> b.id)}
   639	        reminder(assignee, visible_issues, days).deliver_later if visible_issues.present?
   640	      end
   641	    end
   642	  end

Notifications and webhooks are after-commit paths: mail delivery queues jobs with `deliver_later`, while issue/journal model callbacks run inline until the mail/webhook enqueue boundary. I’m doing one final pass for less-obvious dependents: category/status/version/project cleanup, import/migration tasks, and view/render helpers.

    64	  def self.new_statuses_allowed(status, roles, tracker, author=false, assignee=false)
    65	    if roles.present? && tracker
    66	      status_id = status.try(:id) || 0
    67	
    68	      scope = IssueStatus.
    69	        joins(:workflow_transitions_as_new_status).
    70	        where(:workflows => {:old_status_id => status_id, :role_id => roles.map(&:id), :tracker_id => tracker.id})
    71

    73	        if author || assignee
    74	          scope = scope.where("author = ? OR assignee = ?", author, assignee)
    75	        else
    76	          scope = scope.where("author = ? AND assignee = ?", false, false)
    77	        end
    78	      end
    79	
    80	      scope.distinct.to_a.sort
    81	    else
    82	      []
    83	    end

    96	  # Updates issues closed_on attribute when an existing status is set as closed.
    97	  def handle_is_closed_change
    98	    if saved_change_to_is_closed? && is_closed == true
    99	      # First we update issues that have a journal for when the current status was set,
   100	      # a subselect is used to update all issues with a single query
   101	      subquery = Journal.joins(:details).
   102	        where(:journalized_type => 'Issue').
   103	        where("journalized_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id").
   104	        where(:journal_details => {:property => 'attr', :prop_key => 'status_id', :value => id.to_s}).
   105	        select("MAX(created_on)").
   106	        to_sql
   107	      Issue.where(:status_id => id, :closed_on => nil).update_all("closed_on = (#{subquery})")
   108	
   109	      # Then we update issues that don't have a journal which means the
   110	      # current status was set on creation
   111	      Issue.where(:status_id => id, :closed_on => nil).update_all("closed_on = created_on")
   112	    end
   113	  end

   118	    elsif Tracker.where(:default_status_id => id).any?
   119	      raise "This status is used as the default status by some trackers"
   120	    end

The remaining model dependents are mostly cleanup and denormalized/reporting paths: categories nullify or reassign issues, statuses bulk-update `done_ratio`/`closed_on`, project archive/due-date checks issue versions, and imports rebuild parent/relation links after rows are created. I’m pulling helper/rendering and test coverage now, then I’ll produce the ordered JSON summary.

   524	  # Returns the textual representation of a single journal detail
   525	  def show_detail(detail, no_html=false, options={})
   526	    multiple = false
   527	    show_diff = false
   528	    no_details = false
   529	
   530	    case detail.property
   531	    when 'attr'
   532	      field = detail.prop_key.to_s.delete_suffix('_id')
   533	      label = l(("field_#{field}").to_sym)
   534	      case detail.prop_key
   535	      when 'due_date', 'start_date'
   536	        value = format_date(detail.value.to_date) if detail.value
   537	        old_value = format_date(detail.old_value.to_date) if detail.old_value
   538	
   539	      when 'project_id', 'status_id', 'tracker_id', 'assigned_to_id',
   540	            'priority_id', 'category_id', 'fixed_version_id'
   541	        value = find_name_by_reflection(field, detail.value)
   542	        old_value = find_name_by_reflection(field, detail.old_value)
   543	
   544	      when 'estimated_hours'
   545	        value = l_hours_short(detail.value.to_f) unless detail.value.blank?
   546	        old_value = l_hours_short(detail.old_value.to_f) unless detail.old_value.blank?
   547	
   548	      when 'parent_id'
   549	        label = l(:field_parent_issue)
   550	        value = "##{detail.value}" unless detail.value.blank?
   551	        old_value = "##{detail.old_value}" unless detail.old_value.blank?
   552	
   553	      when 'child_id'
   554	        label = l(:label_subtask)
   555	        value = "##{detail.value}" unless detail.value.blank?
   556	        old_value = "##{detail.old_value}" unless detail.old_value.blank?
   557	        multiple = true
   558	
   559	      when 'is_private'
   560	        value = l(detail.value == "0" ? :general_text_No : :general_text_Yes) unless detail.value.blank?
   561	        old_value = l(detail.old_value == "0" ? :general_text_No : :general_text_Yes) unless detail.old_value.blank?
   562	
   563	      when 'description'
   564	        show_diff = true
   565	      end
   566	    when 'cf'
   567	      custom_field = detail.custom_field
   568	      if custom_field
   569	        label = custom_field.name
   570	        if custom_field.format.class.change_no_details
   571	          no_details = true
   572	        elsif custom_field.format.class.change_as_diff
   573	          show_diff = true
   574	        else
   575	          multiple = custom_field.multiple?
   576	          value = format_value(detail.value, custom_field) if detail.value
   577	          old_value = format_value(detail.old_value, custom_field) if detail.old_value
   578	        end
   579	      end
   580	    when 'attachment'
   581	      label = l(:label_attachment)
   582	    when 'relation'
   583	      if detail.value && !detail.old_value
   584	        rel_issue = Issue.visible.find_by_id(detail.value)
   585	        value =
   586	          if rel_issue.nil?
   587	            "#{l(:label_issue)} ##{detail.value}"
   588	          else
   589	            (no_html ? rel_issue : link_to_issue(rel_issue, :only_path => options[:only_path]))
   590	          end
   591	      elsif detail.old_value && !detail.value
   592	        rel_issue = Issue.visible.find_by_id(detail.old_value)
   593	        old_value =
   594	          if rel_issue.nil?
   595	            "#{l(:label_issue)} ##{detail.old_value}"
   596	          else
   597	            (no_html ? rel_issue : link_to_issue(rel_issue, :only_path => options[:only_path]))
   598	          end
   599	      end
   600	      relation_type = IssueRelation::TYPES[detail.prop_key]
   601	      label = l(relation_type[:name]) if relation_type
   602	    end
   603	    call_hook(:helper_issues_show_detail_after_setting,
   604	              {:detail => detail, :label => label, :value => value, :old_value => old_value})
   605	
   606	    label ||= detail.prop_key

   613	      if detail.old_value && detail.value.blank? && detail.property != 'relation'

   616	      if detail.property == 'attachment' && value.present? &&

   792	          tag_options[:title] = object.subject
   793	          has_children =
   794	            if object.leaf?

   796	            else
   797	              children = object.children & project_issues(object.project)

   800	            end
   801	        when Version

   816	          }

   849	        params[:pdf].RDMCell(params[:g_width], 5, "", "LR")
